title: PINACOL AND SEMIPINACOL REARRANGEMENT
summary: Overview page for pinacol and semipinacol rearrangements describing acid- or Lewis-acid-promoted dehydration of vicinal diols or ionization of β-heterosubstituted alcohols to trigger a [1,2]-shift en route to ketones or aldehydes.
family_names: Pinacol and Semipinacol Rearrangement|Pinacol rearrangement|Semipinacol rearrangement
notes: Key concepts: migratory aptitude in carbocation rearrangements, ring expansion or contraction, and mild semipinacol variants that exploit preinstalled leaving groups under highly controlled conditions.

title: PINACOL AND SEMIPINACOL REARRANGEMENT
summary: Application page for pinacol and semipinacol rearrangements featuring (+)-furoscrobiculin B, hydroxyphenstatin, (+)-fredericamycin A, and protomycinolide IV, illustrating ring expansion, vicinal-diol rearrangement, mercury-mediated semipinacol shift, and organoaluminum-triggered alkyl migration in natural-product synthesis.
family_names: Pinacol and Semipinacol Rearrangement|Pinacol rearrangement|Semipinacol rearrangement
notes: Examples emphasize azulenofuran construction from a cis-vicinal diol, rearrangement of an optically active vicinal diol to hydroxyphenstatin, mercuric semipinacol chemistry in spirocenter formation, and DIBALH/Et3Al-promoted [1,2]-alkenyl shifts.

title: PINNER REACTION
summary: Overview page for the Pinner reaction describing acid-catalyzed addition of alcohols or thiols to nitriles to form imino ether or imino thioether hydrohalide salts that can be hydrolyzed to esters or converted to amidinium salts.
notes: Key concepts: dry hydrogen halide activation of nitriles, imino ether hydrochloride intermediates, conversion to amidines, and extension to phenols, monohydric alcohols, and thiols.

title: PINNER REACTION
summary: Application page for the Pinner reaction featuring AI-77B, (±)-sacacarin, and an enantiomerically pure nonpeptidic thrombin inhibitor, highlighting intramolecular ester-forming Pinner cyclization, selective cyano-group activation in a cage diterpenoid, and amidinium-salt generation from aromatic nitriles.
notes: Examples emphasize low-temperature HCl/MeOH conditions, cyclic imino ether hydrochlorides, hydrolysis to acids or esters, and conversion of aromatic nitriles into amidinium hydrochloride motifs for medicinal chemistry.

title: PINNICK OXIDATION
summary: Overview page for the Pinnick oxidation describing buffered sodium chlorite oxidation of aldehydes to carboxylic acids under mild conditions, commonly using NaH2PO4 and an alkene scavenger such as 2-methyl-2-butene to suppress hypochlorous-acid side reactions.
notes: Key concepts: NaClO2-mediated aldehyde oxidation, pH control with phosphate buffer, scavenging of hypochlorous acid, tolerance of α,β-unsaturated aldehydes, and compatibility with diverse sensitive functionality.

title: PINNICK OXIDATION
summary: Application page for the Pinnick oxidation featuring ditryptophenaline, (+)-zaragozic acid C, and (+)-himbacine, illustrating chemoselective aldehyde-to-acid conversion in late-stage alkaloid, polyketide, and terpenoid synthesis without damaging adjacent stereocenters or sensitive unsaturation.
notes: Examples emphasize Dess-Martin plus Pinnick oxidation sequencing, triacid generation en route to zaragozic acid, and preservation of a delicate enyne motif during conversion of an aldehyde-ene fragment into the corresponding acid.

title: POLONOVSKI REACTION
summary: Overview page for the Polonovski reaction describing activation of tertiary amine N-oxides with acylating agents to generate iminium intermediates that fragment or react further to give amides, aldehydes, alkenes, or other α-functionalized amine products.
notes: Key concepts: O-acylation of amine N-oxides, iminium-ion generation, divergent α-C–H functionalization or C–C cleavage pathways, and use of acetic anhydride or trifluoroacetic anhydride in classical and Potier variants.

title: POLONOVSKI REACTION
summary: Application page for the Polonovski reaction featuring serratezomine A, (±)-dynemicin A, (−)-altimicidin, and the core nucleus of FR-900482, showing biomimetic amine oxidation, quinone-imine precursor activation, introduction of a carbamoyl amine unit, and selective oxygenation in mitomycinlike systems.
notes: Examples emphasize mCPBA oxidation to the N-oxide, TFAA-mediated Polonovski-Potier activation, NaBH3CN reduction of iminium intermediates, and installation of oxygenated motifs in densely functionalized alkaloid frameworks.

title: POMERANZ-FRITSCH REACTION
summary: Overview page for the Pomeranz-Fritsch reaction describing condensation of aromatic aldehydes or ketones with 2,2-dialkoxyethylamines to form Schiff bases that cyclize under protic or Lewis acidic conditions to substituted isoquinolines, including Bobbitt and Schlittler-Müller modifications.
notes: Key concepts: benzalaminoacetal formation, acid-triggered isoquinoline ring closure, regioselectivity issues with ortho-unsubstituted aromatic aldehydes, and modified routes to 1-substituted or tetrahydroisoquinoline products.

title: POMERANZ-FRITSCH REACTION
summary: Application page for the Pomeranz-Fritsch reaction featuring the ABFGH subunit of ET 743, (+)-4-hydroxycoryrebanine, papaverine, and (−)-salsolidine, illustrating Bobbitt-modified and asymmetric variants for tetrahydroisoquinoline and isoquinoline construction in alkaloid synthesis.
notes: Examples emphasize enantiopure benzylamine precursors, Schiff-base generation from aminoacetaldehyde diethylacetal, acid-promoted cyclization under 6N HCl or dioxane/HCl, and BzCl-mediated installation of an N-benzyl dihydroisoquinolinium precursor.
